sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Brentford, sitting at 11th in the league with 49 points, host Manchester United, who are 15th with 39 points, on 2025-05-04 at 13:00 at the Brentford Community Stadium in London. This Round 35 clash promises to be intriguing as Brentford aim to capitalize on their higher league position. Manchester United, despite their lower standing, will look to turn their season around in this matchup. The referee for this game is Anthony Taylor, known for averaging 3.8 yellow cards and 0.18 red cards per game. With both teams having something to prove, expect a competitive encounter in the heart of London.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last matchup on 2024-10-19, Manchester United secured a 2-1 victory over Brentford at home. Brentford’s goalkeeper Mark Flekken was the standout with an impressive 8.8 Sofascore rating despite the loss. Over the last 5 encounters between these sides, Manchester United dominate with 3 wins, 1 draw, and no victories for Brentford. Historically, matches between these two often see over 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 meetings. Additionally, head-to-head stats show Manchester United have no losses in their last 4 games against Brentford, while Brentford have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 home games against United.

sections.subtitle: Brentford’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: Brentford come into this game with a streak of no losses in their last 4 general matches. They rank 7th in total goals scored with 58 this season and lead the league in headers scored with 11. Their star player, Bryan Mbeumo, a midfielder, has been exceptional, scoring 18 goals (5th in the league) and creating 16 big chances (4th in the league), with a recent form rating of 7.52 over the last 5 matches. Yoane Wissa, a forward, also contributes with 17 goals and a recent rating of 7.14. Another key figure is Mikkel Damsgaard, ranking 3rd in the league for assists with 10. However, Brentford will miss midfielder Vitaly Janelt due to injury, which might impact their midfield stability.

sections.subtitle: Manchester United’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: Manchester United have shown inconsistency this season, reflected in their 15th position with 39 points. Their recent form in the last 5 matches highlights Casemiro’s brilliance with an 8.1 average Sofascore rating and Bruno Fernandes, who ranks 5th in the league for average rating at 7.59 and leads the league in key passes with 84, boasting a recent form rating of 7.88. Fernandes also stands out in head-to-head stats against Brentford with a 7.74 average rating over the last 5 matchups. Manuel Ugarte has been in form recently with a 7.38 rating. However, United are without key players like Joshua Zirkzee and Lisandro Martínez, which could affect their attacking and defensive balance.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Brentford excel in aerial duels, ranking 2nd in the league with a 52.84% win rate, and in ground duels with a 51.61% win rate (5th). They also convert 44.21% of big chances, leading the league in this metric. However, their possession game lags at 47.53% (14th), and they’ve underperformed their expected goals by 6.34 (16th). Manchester United, on the other hand, score heavily in the latter stages of games, with 14 goals between the 76th and 90th minutes (17.02% of their total). Their away games often see over 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 7 matches, hinting at a physical approach. Both teams have seen over 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 7 head-to-heads, suggesting an open game.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Trends
sections.text: Brentford have the league’s best record for least yellow cards, averaging just 1.56 per game and totaling 53 for the season (1st). They’ve also hit the woodwork 10 times, ranking 13th in terms of unluckiness. Manchester United’s Bruno Fernandes has been a consistent performer against Brentford, starting all 5 of their last encounters with an average rating of 7.74. Brentford have been first to score in 4 of their last 5 home games against United, showing early aggression. Additionally, Brentford’s defensive solidity is evident with only 17.29 opponent shots per game allowed (2nd in the league), which could challenge United’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Referee Impact and Disciplinary Expectations
sections.text: With Anthony Taylor officiating, known for dishing out 3.8 yellow cards per game, discipline will be key for both sides. Brentford’s low yellow card average of 1.56 per game contrasts with head-to-head trends showing over 4.5 cards in 4 of the last 5 meetings between these teams. Manchester United’s recent away games have consistently seen high card counts, with over 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 7. This suggests a potentially feisty encounter, especially with Brentford’s physical style, ranking 19th for most fouls committed with 279 this season.
